articles.title: Stock market today: S&P 500, Nasdaq, Dow futures rise as US and Iran call a halt to attacks
articles.extracted_text: US stock futures climbed on Monday following reports that the US and Iran have agreed to stop tit-for-tat attacks. Nasdaq 100 futures rose 1.2%, S&P 500 futures moved up 0.8%, Dow futures put on roughly 0.4%. Oil prices moved higher in early morning: Brent up 0.6% at $73/bbl, WTI up 0.9% at just below $70. S&P 500 and Nasdaq booked weekly losses of over 2% and 4.5% respectively on Friday. Nvidia and Alphabet both saw shares slide over 8% last week. Dow eked out 0.6% gain. Highlight of coming week: June jobs report due Thursday (not Friday). Markets closed Friday for Fourth of July holiday.

articles.title: Oil Prices Rise as U.S. and Iran Reach Deal to Halt Attacks
articles.extracted_text: WTI Aug '26 contract: $69.88/bbl, +$0.84 (+1.21%). Brent crude: $72.45/bbl, up 0.6%. WTI had closed below $70 on Friday for first time since Feb. 27 (day before Iran war started). U.S. and Iran agreed to stand down and allow free transit through Strait of Hormuz. Saturday: Panamanian-flagged tanker M/T Kiku struck while transiting strait with 2M+ barrels. Kuwait and Bahrain reported incoming missiles. U.S. struck 10 Iranian military targets. Sunday: VP Vance arrived in Switzerland for talks with Iranian delegation. Trump posted on Truth Social threatening to 'militarily complete the job.' ING analysts: 'complacency is odd and leaves significant upside risk if supply recovery proves slow.'

articles.title: U.S. and Iran Agree to Stand Down, Resume Talks After Days of Hostilities
articles.extracted_text: MOU signed June 17 established 60-day negotiation period. Strait of Hormuz dispute unresolved — Iran asserts right to control the strait, U.S. flatly rejects. Thursday: Iranian projectile hit Singapore-flagged cargo vessel. Friday: U.S. CENTCOM fresh strikes on drone sites, coastal radar, missile infrastructure. Saturday: Panama-flagged tanker hit by Iranian drone; U.S. retaliated; Iran launched missiles at Kuwait and Bahrain bases. Sunday: Bahrain residential building damaged; Kuwait intercepted 2 ballistic missiles. No casualties. U.S. and Iran agreed to stop attacks and resume negotiations Tuesday in Qatar. Talks shifted from nuclear program to Strait of Hormuz. Israel continued Lebanon attacks over weekend.

articles.title: U.S. Dollar Index Continuous Contract Overview
Stocks making the biggest moves premarket: CMCSA, SPCX, CHTR, IRDM, RKLB, GOOGL, ORCL, WULF, MLM, VZ
articles.extracted_text: Comcast +20%: spinning off NBCUniversal/Sky media portfolio, ~1yr timeline. Co-CEO Cavanagh to lead NBCU, ex-CFO Angelakis to lead remaining telecom. Rocket Lab +10%: acquiring Iridium Communications (+20%). SpaceX +2%: added to Nasdaq 100 before July 7 trading, ETF buying expected. Charter Communications +20%: Bloomberg report of exclusive SpaceX consumer phone talks. Alphabet +1%: first day on Dow Jones Industrial Average, replacing Verizon (-0.5%). Oracle +3%: rebounding from -19% worst weekly drop since 2001 on debt/AI concerns. TeraWulf +3%: Citi initiated buy, data center power delivery play. Martin Marietta -3%: $13.5B cash acquisition of Lhoist North America. Verizon: Q2 projected losses $700M-$800M from BT Group JV classification.

articles.title: What to Look Out for in Economic Data This Week (June 29-July 3)
articles.extracted_text: Holiday-shortened week (markets closed Friday July 3 for Independence Day). Monday: Dallas Fed Mfg (9:30 AM ET). Tuesday: JOLTS May, Nike earnings. Wednesday: ADP Employment, ISM Mfg PMI, Fed Chair Warsh speaks, ECB Lagarde, BoE Bailey, BoC Macklem. Thursday: June Jobs Report (released Thursday not Friday due to holiday). Consensus expectations not provided in excerpt.

articles.title: Newsquawk Weekly Economic Calendar — 29th June - 3rd July 2026
articles.extracted_text: Monday: Japanese Retail Sales, Spanish CPI Prelim, BoE Pill, ECB Lagarde. Tuesday: Japan Unemployment, China NBS PMIs, German CPI/Retail Sales, UK GDP Final Q1, French CPI Prelim, Canada GDP Apr, US JOLTS May, Nike earnings, RBA Minutes. Wednesday: Japan Tankan Q2, Global Mfg PMI Final, EU CPI Prelim, US ADP Employment, ISM Mfg PMI, Atlanta Fed GDP Q2, Fed Warsh, ECB Lagarde/Vujcic/Cipollone/Lane, BoE Bailey, BoC Macklem. Thursday: Australia Trade Balance, EU Unemployment, US Jobs Report June, Initial Jobless Claims. Friday: US holiday (Independence Day observed).

articles.title: Nasdaq Composite posts fifth losing session Friday as chip stocks tumble
articles.extracted_text: S&P 500 -0.1%, Nasdaq -0.2% Friday. Fifth straight decline for both. Weekly: S&P -2%, Nasdaq -4.5%. Dow +0.6% for week (healthcare support). Healthcare sector +7% for the week — biggest weekly advance since June 2022. Magnificent Seven: NVDA and GOOGL both down 8%+ for week. Oracle -19% worst week since 2001 on debt/AI concerns.

articles.title: NVIDIA (NVDA) Pre-Market Trading Summary
articles.extracted_text: NVDA pre-market: $194.56, +$2.03 (+1.05%) from Friday close of $192.53. Pre-market range: $194.65-$194.69.

articles.title: Strategy (MSTR) Signals New Bitcoin Purchases Amidst Market Pressure
articles.extracted_text: MSTR acquired 520 BTC for ~$35M. Controls 847,363 BTC valued at ~$50.8B. Faces ~$13B in unrealized losses (avg purchase price ~$75,646/BTC). MSTR stock at ~$82 — valuation has fallen below value of bitcoin holdings (mNAV below 1). Saylor hinted at more BTC purchases Monday. BTC trading at ~$59,964.

articles.title: CBOE Volatility Index (VIX)
articles.extracted_text: VIX spot: 18.33 (-0.43%). Friday close: 18.41. Thursday June 25: 18.89. VIX declining as Iran ceasefire reduces tail-risk premium.

articles.title: Stock Market News, June 26, 2026: Nasdaq Edges Lower to Close Tough Week
articles.extracted_text: U.S. indexes finished Friday down slightly, a fifth-straight decline for S&P 500 and Nasdaq. Tech stocks faced pressure again, with chip stocks leading declines. S&P 500 closed at 7,354.02 (-0.05%). Nasdaq at 25,297.62 (-0.24%). Dow at 51,876.11 (-0.09%).

articles.title: Fed Holds Rates and Leans Toward Fighting Inflation — Warsh Era Begins
articles.extracted_text: FOMC voted unanimously to keep benchmark rate at 3.5%-3.75% on June 17. Fed Chair Kevin Warsh: hawkish lean, odds of rate hike this year >85% in futures markets. Polymarket: 78% chance of 0 cuts in 2026. Warsh vowed 'new chapter' in Fed oversight.

articles.title: May 2026 Jobs Report: Nonfarm Payrolls +172K, Unemployment 4.3%
articles.extracted_text: May NFP: +172,000 vs consensus 85,000. Unemployment rate: 4.3% unchanged. Prior month revised up to +179,000. Unemployment rate held in 4.3%-4.5% range since July 2025. Leisure/hospitality, local government, health care led gains. June report due Thursday July 2.

articles.title: Analyst Upgrades and Downgrades for Monday, 6/29
articles.extracted_text: 2 upgrades, 7 downgrades, 5 price target revisions on June 29. AMR (Alpha Metallurgical): Jefferies Hold $165. LFTO (Liftoff Mobile): Needham Buy $42. LQDA (Liquidia): BofA Securities Neutral $79. SEI (Solaris Energy Infrastructure): Needham Buy $97.
